contextBlurb: New York has the most developed stablecoin regulatory framework of any U.S. state. The BitLicense regime (23 NYCRR Part 200, 2015) requires licensure for virtual currency businesses, and NYDFS issued binding guidance in June 2022 setting specific requirements for USD-backed stablecoins: 1:1 reserve backing, independent monthly audits, and unconditional redemption rights.

legislation.title: Regulation of Virtual Currency Businesses (BitLicense)
legislation.summary: Establishes a licensing regime for virtual currency businesses operating in New York, including reserve, capital, and consumer protection requirements.

legislation.title: Guidance on the Issuance of U.S. Dollar-Backed Stablecoins
legislation.summary: NYDFS binding guidance requiring USD-backed stablecoins to maintain 1:1 reserves, undergo independent monthly audits, and guarantee unconditional redemption at par.
